title: Drip vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Drip vs Omnisend: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between Drip and Omnisend comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Drip is built for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Omnisend targets e-commerce brands wanting email and SMS together. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-Commerce Businesses Wanting Deep Store Integration
comparison_table.tool_b: E-Commerce Brands Wanting Email And Sms Together

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on e-commerce brands wanting email and SMS together

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Drip wins for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration, while Omnisend is stronger for e-commerce brands wanting email and SMS together. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend starts at a lower price point ($16/mo vs $39/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Omnisend to Drip is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
